Operation 'getTransactionStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Current description is too minimal. It doesn't clarify what status information is returned, what the transaction_id parameter represents, or when an AI agent should use this operation. Enhanced description specifies return data structure, clarifies the lookup mechanism, and provides context for agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Retrieves the status of a completed transaction
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status and details of a transaction by transaction ID. Returns transaction status information including payment method, amount, timestamp, and completion state. Use this operation to verify transaction outcomes after payment processing.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. message: Operation 'processTransaction' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is generic. Description lacks critical details about inputs (what transaction details are required), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (funds are charged/transferred). Enhanced version clarifies the three payment methods, specifies input requirements, details the response structure, and explicitly notes the financial side effect for AI agent awareness.

data.current_description: Processes card payments, mobile money transactions, or fund transfers
data.summary_suggestion: Process payment via card, mobile money, or bank transfer
data.description_suggestion: Processes a payment transaction using one of three methods: card payment, mobile money transaction, or bank transfer. Accepts transaction details including amount, customer information, and payment method type. Returns transaction processing result with status, reference ID, and any required next steps (such as OTP verification or authorization). This operation directly charges the customer's account or initiates fund movement.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description lacks critical details about inputs (what transaction details are required), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (funds are charged/transferred). Enhanced version clarifies the three payment methods, specifies input requirements, details the response structure, and explicitly notes the financial side effect for AI agent awareness.

message: Operation 'authorizeBankTransfer' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_context. Current description mentions 'name enquiry validation' but doesn't explain what inputs are required or what the operation returns. Enhanced description clarifies the workflow dependency (requires prior name enquiry), specifies input requirements (authorization token/reference), details the output (confirmation with reference), and provides operational context for AI agents to understand this is a workflow-dependent operation.

data.current_description: Completes a bank transfer after name enquiry validation
data.description_suggestion: Completes a bank transfer by authorizing a previously validated bank account. Requires the authorization token or reference from a prior bank name enquiry operation. Accepts authorization credentials and completes the fund transfer to the validated recipient account. Returns transfer confirmation with transaction reference and status. This is the final step in the bank transfer workflow after account validation.
data.reasoning: Current description mentions 'name enquiry validation' but doesn't explain what inputs are required or what the operation returns. Enhanced description clarifies the workflow dependency (requires prior name enquiry), specifies input requirements (authorization token/reference), details the output (confirmation with reference), and provides operational context for AI agents to understand this is a workflow-dependent operation.

message: Operation 'reverseCardTransaction' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is minimal and lacks critical details. Enhanced version specifies required inputs (transaction ID, reason), clarifies the financial flow (merchant debited, customer credited), details the output (reversal reference), explicitly notes the side effects (fund movement), and provides context about processing timelines. This helps AI agents understand when and why to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Reverses a previously processed card transaction
data.description_suggestion: Reverses a previously processed card transaction, returning funds to the customer's card. Requires the original transaction ID and reversal reason. Returns reversal confirmation with new reversal reference ID and status. This operation debits the merchant account and credits the customer's card - use only for legitimate reversals such as duplicate charges or customer cancellations. Reversal processing may take 1-3 business days to appear on the customer's statement.
data.reasoning: Current description is minimal and lacks critical details.
